title: Enrichment worker hardening and next steps

segments.text: I think the silent fallback on invalid source_type in the enrichment worker is wrong. If the payload is corrupted, the worker should fail the job instead of substituting the loaded artifact type.
Agreed. Silent fallback hides corruption and could route to the wrong processor later once artifact types diverge.
Also, a completed derivation run should not have completed_at set to null. That is just inconsistent state.
Yes. If run_status is completed, completed_at should be stamped at write time.
We also need a real test for the artifact-not-found path. That branch currently exists in the worker but isn't covered properly.
That should be added as a focused worker test: claimed job, load_artifact_for_enrichment returns None, fail_job is called, and no derived writes occur.
After that, task 12 is next. The processor seam is in place, and the next milestone is replacing the stub with a real enrichment path.
